commands.review_summary:
Adds an optional `resumable` boolean to Gateway and agent-facing session rows, passes it through `sessions_list`, and updates session tool/prompt wording plus snapshots and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +5. Total +25 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection, not by a live run in this read-only review. Current main exposes terminal-looking `status` values without `resumable`, while `sessions_send` can reactivate completed subagent sessions after a successful send.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof; there is no safe automated code repair lane for proving the contributor's runtime setup.

target.title: fix: add resumable field to prevent duplicate session spawns
target.body: ## Problem Session `status` field values (`done`, `failed`, `timeout`, `killed`) mislead agents into treating sessions as permanently terminated, causing duplicate `sessions_spawn` calls instead of resuming existing sessions via `sessions_send`. Closes #64103 ## Root Cause The `status` field reflects **last-turn communication state**, not session lifecycle state. However, nothing in the data model or tool descriptions makes this distinction explicit — so LLM agents interpret `failed`/`timeout`/`done` as terminal and spawn new sessions. `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession()` in `session-subagent-reactivation.ts` confirms that sessions in these states **are** resumable via `sessions_send`. ## Solution (Incremental, backward-compatible) Following [ClawSweeper's review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64103#issuecomment-*) recommendation of additive metadata over breaking rename: 1. **Add `resumable?: boolean` field** to `GatewaySessionRow` and `SessionListRow` — non-running sessions set `resumable: true` 2. **Compute in `buildGatewaySessionRow()`** — `resolvedStatus !== "running"` → `resumable: true`; running sessions omit it 3. **Pass through in `sessions-list-tool`** — agent sees `resumable` in output 4. **Update tool description** — `describeSessionsListTool()` now explains status semantics 5. **Update system prompt** — `sessions_list` summary clarifies status is last-turn only, prefer `sessions_send` ## Changed Files | File | Change | |---|---| | `src/gateway/session-utils.types.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `GatewaySessionRow` | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `SessionListRow` | | `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` | Compute `resolvedStatus` + `resolvedResumable` in `buildGatewaySessionRow()` | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` | Pass `resumable` through to agent output | | `src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts` | Add status semantics note to `describeSessionsListTool()` | | `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` | Add status guidance to `sessions_list` tool summary | | `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` | Add `resumable: undefined` to test expectation | | `src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts` | Add `resumable` assertions for done/failed/timeout/running states | ## Test Results ``` 12 test files passed, 194 tests passed oxfmt format check: all passed Prompt snapshots regenerated ``` ## Real behavior proof Verified via test assertions that `resumable` is correctly computed for each session status: - `status: "running"` → `resumable: undefined` (session is active, no need to signal resumability) - `status: "done"` → `resumable: true` (session can still receive `sessions_send`) - `status: "failed"` → `resumable: true` (session can still receive `sessions_send`) - `status: "timeout"` → `resumable: true` (session can still receive `sessions_send`) Test suite `session-utils.subagent.test.ts` confirms all four mappings pass with 81 tests (0 failures).
target.author: w1ndcn
